name: Chase Sapphire Preferred
rewardsSummary: 3x on dining, 2x on travel, 1x on everything else.
highlights: Transfer partners: United, Southwest, Hyatt, Marriott
notes: Best for frequent travelers who value transferable points over simplicity.

name: Chase Sapphire Reserve
rewardsSummary: 10x on Chase travel, 3x on dining, 3x on other travel, 1x on everything else.
notes: Best premium travel card for high spenders who maximize lounge and travel credits.

name: American Express Gold Card
rewardsSummary: 4x at restaurants, 4x at US supermarkets (up to $25k/yr), 3x on flights, 1x on other.
highlights: $120 annual dining credit (Grubhub, Cheesecake Factory, etc.)
Membership Rewards points transferable to 20+ airlines
signupBonus: 60,000 Membership Rewards points after $6,000 spend in 6 months
notes: Best card for foodies and grocery spenders who can use the dining/Uber credits.

name: The Platinum Card from American Express
rewardsSummary: 5x on flights booked directly or via Amex Travel, 5x on prepaid hotels via Amex Travel, 1x on other.
signupBonus: 80,000 Membership Rewards points after $8,000 spend in 6 months
notes: Best ultra-premium travel card for luxury travelers who can use all the credits.

name: Citi Double Cash Card
rewardsSummary: 2% cash back on all purchases (1% when you buy + 1% when you pay).
highlights: Flat 2% on everything — no category tracking needed
0% intro APR on balance transfers for 18 months
No spending caps or rotating categories
notes: Best no-fee flat-rate cashback card for simplicity-seekers.

name: Chase Freedom Unlimited
rewardsSummary: 5% on Chase travel, 3% on dining and drugstores, 1.5% on all other purchases.
highlights: 1.5% flat on everything (better than most no-fee cards)
$200 cash bonus after $500 spend in first 3 months
0% intro APR for 15 months on purchases
Pairs well with Chase Sapphire cards for point transfers
notes: Excellent everyday no-fee card, especially as a pairing card in the Chase ecosystem.

name: Capital One Venture X Rewards Credit Card
rewardsSummary: 10x on hotels and rental cars via Capital One Travel, 5x on flights via Capital One Travel, 2x on everything else.
highlights: Priority Pass + Capital One Lounge access
Up to $100 Global Entry/TSA PreCheck credit
notes: Best mid-tier premium travel card — effective annual fee often net-zero after credits.

name: Capital One Quicksilver Cash Rewards
rewardsSummary: 1.5% unlimited cash back on all purchases.
highlights: No foreign transaction fees (rare for a no-fee card)
$200 cash bonus after $500 spend in 3 months
notes: Best no-fee cashback card for fair-credit applicants who travel internationally.

name: Wells Fargo Active Cash Card
rewardsSummary: Unlimited 2% cash rewards on all purchases.
highlights: Unlimited 2% cash rewards — no caps, no categories
$200 cash rewards bonus after $500 spend in 3 months
0% intro APR for 15 months on purchases and qualifying balance transfers
introApr: 0% on purchases and balance transfers for 15 months
notes: Top competitor to Citi Double Cash — same 2% flat rate with a better welcome bonus.

name: Blue Cash Preferred Card from American Express
rewardsSummary: 6% at US supermarkets (up to $6k/yr), 6% on select US streaming, 3% on transit and gas, 1% on other.
highlights: $84 annual Disney Bundle credit (offsets most of annual fee)
$250 welcome bonus after $3,000 spend in 6 months
notes: Best card for families with high grocery and streaming spending.

name: Apple Card
rewardsSummary: 3% on Apple purchases and select merchants, 2% on Apple Pay purchases, 1% on all other.
highlights: No annual fee, no late fees, no foreign transaction fees
Daily Cash deposited directly to Apple Cash
Accessible to applicants with fair credit
notes: Best for Apple ecosystem users; also one of the most accessible premium-feel cards.

name: Discover it Student Cash Back
rewardsSummary: 5% cash back in rotating quarterly categories (up to $1,500/quarter), 1% on all other purchases.
highlights: No annual fee, no late fee on first late payment
Cashback Match — Discover doubles all cash back earned in year 1
Designed for students with no/limited credit history
No credit score required to apply
signupBonus: Cashback Match at end of first year
notes: Best first credit card for students with no credit history.

name: U.S. Bank Altitude Go Visa Signature Card
rewardsSummary: 4x on dining (including takeout and delivery), 2x on groceries and gas, 1x on all other.
highlights: 4x on dining — one of the best dining rates among no-fee cards
20,000 point bonus after $1,000 spend in 90 days
notes: Best no-fee dining card — ideal for restaurant-heavy spenders.

name: Citi Simplicity Card
rewardsSummary: No rewards — designed for balance transfers and debt payoff.
highlights: 0% intro APR on balance transfers for 21 months (one of the longest available)
0% intro APR on purchases for 12 months
No late fees, no penalty APR
introApr: 0% on balance transfers for 21 months; 0% on purchases for 12 months
notes: Best card for paying down existing credit card debt — longest 0% balance transfer period.

name: Ink Business Cash Credit Card
rewardsSummary: 5% on office supplies and internet/cable/phone (up to $25k/yr), 2% on gas and dining, 1% on other.
highlights: $750 cash back welcome bonus after $6,000 spend in 3 months
Employee cards at no extra cost
Pairs with Chase Sapphire cards to convert to transferable points
notes: Best no-fee business card for office supply and telecom spenders.
